steps.title: Identify the pressure‑fluctuation clue
steps.content:
The passage states that as vortex size grows, pressure fluctuations at the snout increase for narrow‑headed and intermediate‑headed fish but stay low for wide‑headed fish.
This tells us which head shapes experience high versus low fluctuations when vortices are large.

steps.title: Apply the clue to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice C shows that, with large vortices in low visibility, the wide‑headed Synodontis macropunctata bumped into obstructions more often than the intermediate‑headed dusky smooth‑hound did.

Since wide‑headed fish have low pressure fluctuations and intermediate‑headed fish have high fluctuations under those conditions, this pattern — more bumping (less avoidance) when fluctuations are low and less bumping (more avoidance) when fluctuations are high — directly matches the hypothesis that greater snout pressure fluctuations make a fish more likely to avoid an obstruction.
The other three choices either give no comparison between high‑ and low‑fluctuation groups, report data for only one group, or look at variation within a single group, none of which directly tests the fluctuation‑avoidance link.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
